objective: Deliver the first runnable M07 vertical slice: a typed avatar state machine and loopback control-plane API, direct observation of real conversation turn states, real TTS playback-driven mouth motion, and an owner-facing Avatar Stage dashboard page using an original code-native visual fallback while VRM/Electron asset and dependency provenance are prepared.
non_goals: Do not claim that the code-native fallback is a VRM, Live2D model, finished production avatar or replacement for the later Electron/Vue stage shell.
Do not add an unlicensed avatar, motion, texture, voice likeness or copied frontend artwork.
Do not let the renderer read SQLite, Qdrant, model files or provider internals directly.
Do not accept avatar cues from public, viewer, web, OCR, VLM or other untrusted sources.
Do not invent phoneme alignment: TTS lip motion in this slice is driven by observed audio amplitude and is labeled accordingly.
Do not run repeat, soak, dropped-frame or eight-hour release workflows during this fast-development iteration.
contracts: Avatar states are exactly idle, listening, thinking, speaking, interrupted and error; every accepted cue produces a monotonic bounded audit-safe snapshot without conversation text.
Invalid or missing emotion cues fall back to neutral; viseme and intensity inputs are allowlisted and bounded.
Conversation state is forwarded by the runtime service through a typed callback; the renderer never infers backend turn state by reading model, database or provider internals.
Only trusted local sources may mutate avatar state; public and untrusted sources fail closed with a stable error.
The code-native avatar manifest identifies original repository-owned artwork and the UI explicitly reports vrmLoaded false until a licensed VRM asset is installed.
The browser reports observed TTS playback start, pause and end through typed loopback cues; mouth magnitude is derived from real Web Audio samples and never represented as phoneme-accurate alignment.
Mute and emergency-stop controls use the existing safety control plane and remain visibly distinguishable from cosmetic avatar preview controls.
All M07 endpoints bind through the existing loopback server and return stable JSON error codes without raw user or model text.
allowed_dependencies: Python 3.12+ standard library and existing Hina runtime, contracts, text-brain and speech packages.
Existing dependency-free HTML, CSS, SVG and JavaScript Dev Console.
Browser Web Audio API for local analysis of the WAV already returned by Hina's TTS endpoint.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests execute at least 1000 deterministic requested avatar state changes without a stuck terminal state and prove reset recovery.
Unit tests prove invalid emotion falls back to neutral, amplitude is clamped, history is bounded and untrusted sources are rejected.
Conversation tests prove listening, thinking, speaking, idle, interrupted and error changes reach the avatar callback without raw text.
Runtime tests cover avatar status, cue and reset endpoints with stable success and rejection semantics.
Dev Console exposes a dedicated Avatar Stage route with plain Vietnamese purpose, usage and limitation notes suitable for a non-developer.
The stage renders real runtime state and safety status; operator state preview is labeled as a manual preview rather than backend activity.
Playing a real TTS WAV drives mouth motion through Web Audio amplitude and sends speaking/idle playback cues to the runtime.
Fast avatar unit, text-brain unit, core runtime unit, JavaScript syntax and Dev Console startup smoke pass once.
Manual browser verification confirms fixed dashboard navigation, responsive stage layout, real API updates and no console errors.
required_test_commands: uv run --frozen python -m unittest discover -s apps/core-runtime/tests -p "test_*.py"
